briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 20, 2026. Today, we will primarily cover news regarding the intensifying climate crisis phenomena both domestically and internationally, various efforts to address them, and the acceleration of renewable energy transition.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensifying Climate Crisis: Global Impacts and Warnings
sections.content: Recently, extreme weather phenomena such as heatwaves, droughts, and torrential downpours are intensifying daily both domestically and internationally [13, 32, 35, 162, 164, 165, 169, 179, 180]. Over 60% of U.S. voters recognize these as impacts of the climate crisis [15]. These changes are having severe impacts across ecosystems and agriculture, including exceeding tuna fishing quotas in the East Sea [4], the appearance of sharks [14], a crisis for the export-leading seaweed industry [2], the loss of traditional Amazonian wisdom [27], the risk of losing wild species in Iraq [172], concerns for Greek pistachio farmers [178], and increased pressure on European forests [190]. Furthermore, increased fine dust pollution in Europe due to sandstorms originating from the Sahara [17] and a Swiss study finding that climate change exacerbates ozone pollution [171] highlight the severity of air quality issues. Domestic climate organizations are urging the implementation of the Constitutional Court's decision and emphasizing that the amendment of the Carbon Neutrality Act should no longer be delayed [88].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 A Wave of Climate Action: Strengthening Domestic and International Policies and Cooperation
sections.content: Domestic and international cooperation and policy efforts to address the climate crisis are active. The World Heritage Committee adopted the 'Busan Declaration,' emphasizing cooperation on climate change and armed conflict, and urged global awareness [7, 9, 11, 19, 25, 36, 182]. As the Conference of the Parties on Climate Change opened in the UK, the Republic of Korea government also expressed its commitment to carbon neutrality [8]. Domestically, the National Assembly Climate Change Forum discussed resolving bottlenecks for power grid expansion [1, 33], and the Korea Offshore & Shipbuilding Association has commenced proactive research on climate change response [3, 6]. At the corporate level, Sampyo Cement published an ESG report with climate change response and industrial safety as key issues [23, 24, 29, 37, 72, 76, 90, 94, 97], while Samsung Display and LG Display are accelerating their ESG responses in line with their clients' 2030 carbon neutrality demands [39, 60, 46, 89]. Local governments are also leading the transition to a carbon-neutral society by operating various practical lifestyle programs, such as Gwacheon City's establishment of a carbon-neutral local energy plan [40, 41, 42, 63, 69, 79, 87, 93, 95], Chungnam Provincial Office of Education's introduction of carbon-neutral meetings [43], Gimhae City's expansion of carbon-neutral education for infants and toddlers [57], and Hwasun County's provision of local currency for carbon-neutral practices [49, 61, 82].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition: Power Grid Innovation and Market Trends
sections.content: Discussions on stabilizing the power system have earnestly begun in preparation for the era of 100GW renewable energy. The Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment launched the 'Grid Code Expert Committee' and commenced the revision of power grid technical standards [106, 107, 108, 112, 113, 114, 115, 119, 140, 146, 150, 158, 160]. KEPCO KDN is leveraging its ICT capabilities to resolve challenges in renewable energy connection, dedicating full efforts to stabilizing the national power grid [143]. The Shinan-Uii offshore wind power project, the largest in Korea, has broken ground [67]. The approval of Korea Midland Power's Haenam Hwawon BESS project [121, 123] and the completion of Korea Southern Power's Jeju Andeok BESS [129, 155, 161] are expected to alleviate renewable energy output curtailment issues in the Honam and Jeju regions. A surge in international oil prices led to a strong performance of renewable energy-related stocks, improving investment sentiment [122, 130, 135, 141, 142, 144, 151, 153, 157]. Enerdot is expanding its overseas market presence by introducing AI-based renewable energy operation and management solutions in Vietnam [111, 125, 128, 136, 148]. However, concerns about disruptions to the power system are also being raised, such as the surging energy demand in Gwacheon's 3rd new town [134] and the stability of power supply for AI data centers [147, 154], necessitating continuous attention and the search for solutions [133].

sections.title: 📰 Diverse Aspects of Climate Change: Social Perception and Challenges
sections.content: Climate change is having widespread impacts across society, culture, and technology, extending beyond mere environmental issues. Just as Amazonian wisdom is at risk of disappearing [27], climate change threatens traditional crafts [188] and makes the protection of World Heritage sites even more challenging [11, 19, 170, 182]. The international community is making diplomatic efforts to secure funding for climate crisis response [166], and Costa Rica is sparking global reconsideration by hosting a sustainable architecture congress [181]. Meanwhile, alongside efforts to enhance scientific understanding of how climate models predict climate change [22], vigilance is also needed against information distortion phenomena such as fake news about heatwaves [20] or AI-manipulated images in birdwatching forums [195]. Ultimately, the recognition that knowledge is the best defense against climate change is spreading [193], and in Africa, various approaches are being taken to respond to climate change, including utilizing science to combat climate-related health threats [189].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing clearly illustrated the various signs of the globally intensifying climate crisis, the proactive efforts across different sectors to combat it, and the opportunities and challenges in the process of renewable energy transition. We hope that our efforts to overcome the climate crisis will bear fruit through continued attention and cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.
